pre_existing_findings.summary: Campaign layer (16 files, ~4000+ lines) has zero test coverage — no test directory, no imports from @/campaign in any test file
pre_existing_findings.note: Partially outdated — truth-closure.test.ts and _quick-import.test.ts exist inside the campaign dir

internal_helpers.description: SHA256 hash of ordered binder fields for tamper-evident digest
Returns a fresh Binder with default empty arrays and digest computed
Immutable evidence append — spreads into the correct section by name (switch on section)
Persists a binder.* event to EventStore for audit trail
Replays all binder.* events from EventStore to rebuild binder state

lane_state_fields.note: --- SHADOW FIELD --- Cached binder reference; updated in processEvent terminal block

createBinder_call_sites.context: Called unconditionally when creating a new lane. Creates binder with identical mission and scope values (4th param = scope, NOT mission). Creates a new lane ID via Identifier.recipe().

Called when isTerminal(newState) is true — i.e., state is "returned", "failed", or "blocked". Creates the binder with lane.scope as both mission AND scope params (identical). Followed immediately by addEvidence for transition and event stream, then updateStatus.

Called ONLY when binderService.getBinder(laneId) returns None. Conditionally creates a binder if one doesn't exist yet for this lane. This is a defensive/backfill create — the primary creation should have happened at createLane or processEvent terminal block.

ensureBinder_call_sites.note: NO external callers found. ensureBinder is defined at binder.ts:436 but is only used internally within the binder service's make function. The secretary NEVER calls ensureBinder — it always calls createBinder instead.

addEvidence_call_sites.context: Adds terminal transition evidence with section 'transitionEvents'
Adds event stream evidence with section 'executionEvents'
Calls binderService.addEvidence(laneId, section, evidencePayload) after building the correct typed payload

updateStatus_call_sites.context: Updates binder status to newState as BinderLaneState

finalizeBinder_call_sites.context: Called when output.role === 'historian' AND updatedLane.currentState === 'checkpointed'. This is the ONLY call to finalizeBinder outside binder.ts.

getBinder_call_sites.context: During lane initialization/replay — reconstructs binder from EventStore
Checks if binder already exists before potentially creating one
Falls back to EventStore when in-memory lane.binder is null

reconstructBinder_call_sites.context: Falls back to full EventStore replay when lane.binder is null

binder_shadow_state_pattern.description: LaneState.binder (secretary.ts:124) is a cached reference to the canonical Binder object. It shadows the EventStore-backed binder in the BinderService. This creates a dual-source-of-truth problem: the in-memory lane.binder may diverge from the EventStore-reconstructed binder.

write_locations.note: binder is set on the lane state object via Ref.update on activeLanes map. The binder is constructed from binderService.getBinder and set as { ...existing, binder: newBinder }.

binder_shadow_state_pattern.divergence_risk: The in-memory lane.binder is set during processEvent terminal block based on what binderService.getBinder(laneId) returns at that moment. If addRoleEvidence later calls binderService.addEvidence without updating the in-memory lane.binder, the shadow will be stale. getLaneBinder reads lane.binder first (memory), then falls back to EventStore only if null — meaning a non-null but stale shadow will be returned.

createLane.summary: Generates a new lane ID via Identifier.recipe(), creates a binder via binderService.createBinder(id, campaignId, scope, scope), initializes an empty LaneState in activeLanes Ref, and returns the lane ID. NOTE: binder is NOT stored on the initial LaneState (lane.binder is null).

processEvent_terminal.summary: When isTerminal(newState): (1) creates binder via createBinder, (2) adds transitionEvents evidence, (3) adds executionEvents evidence, (4) updates binder status, (5) updates LaneState.binder in Ref, (6) updates LaneState in Ref with new state and event stream.

addRoleEvidence.summary: 1. Ensures lane exists. 2. Checks if binder exists (creates if not). 3. Skips on failure/blocked outputs. 4. Maps role→section via switch (scout/cartographer→scoutReports, architect→architecturePlan, critic→criticReviews, executor→executionEvents, validator→validationResults, redteam→redTeamFindings, historian→handoffSummary). 5. Builds correctly-typed evidence payload per section. 6. Calls binderService.addEvidence(laneId, section, payload). NOTE: Does NOT update in-memory lane.binder shadow.

handleRoleOutput.summary: 1. Calls addRoleEvidence(laneId, output). 2. Converts output to RuntimeEvent via roleOutputToEvent. 3. Calls processEvent(laneId, event) to advance state machine. 4. SPECIAL CASE: if role is 'historian' and lane state is 'checkpointed', emits LaneReturned event AND calls binderService.finalizeBinder(laneId).

getLaneBinder.summary: 1. Ensures lane exists. 2. Checks lane.binder (in-memory shadow) — if exists and state is terminal, returns immediately. 3. If not terminal, fails with LaneNotTerminalError. 4. Falls back to binderService.getBinder(laneId).

returnLane.summary: 1. Ensures lane exists. 2. Checks lane.binder (in-memory shadow) — returns immediately if found. 3. Falls back to binderService.reconstructBinder(laneId) (full EventStore replay). 4. Returns reconstructed binder or fails.

isTerminal.note: These three states trigger the terminal binder creation block in processEvent

binder_ts_importers.imports: Service as BinderService, type Binder as CanonicalBinder, layer as BinderLayer, BinderError, type LaneState as BinderLaneState

secretary_ts_importers.imports: secretaryDef (from ./secretary — this is agent/machine/secretary.ts, NOT campaign/secretary.ts)
export * as SecretaryMachine from ./secretary (agent machine def, NOT campaign)

secretary_ts_depends_on: effect (Context, Effect, Layer, Option, Ref, Schema)

context_packet_ts.note: References a 'binderArtifactHashes' field on LaneContext type — NOT from binder.ts, but from its own LaneContext interface (line 21)

references.note: References fields on PushGateContext interface — NOT directly from binder.ts
Uses its OWN Binder interface (lines 36-46) which is a different type from binder.ts Binder. It imports BinderService for creating/accessing binders but defines its own Binder type.

smoking_guns.title: createBinder has identical mission and scope params at all 3 call sites
smoking_guns.detail: All 3 call sites pass scope as the 4th param: `binderService.createBinder(id, campaignId, scope, scope)`. The Interface signature is (laneId, campaignId, mission, scope). This means missionObjective is always set to the same value as laneScope — the mission objective is never passed.

smoking_guns.title: ensureBinder is defined but NEVER called from any external module
smoking_guns.detail: ensureBinder at binder.ts:436 has ZERO external callers. The secretary always uses createBinder, which returns a string (NOT a Binder). This means the idempotent 'get-or-create' semantic is available but unused — every call creates a new binder event in the EventStore.

smoking_guns.title: Binder shadow state can go stale after addRoleEvidence
smoking_guns.detail: LaneState.binder (secretary.ts:124) is set during processEvent terminal block but NOT updated by addRoleEvidence, which calls binderService.addEvidence without updating the in-memory lane.binder. Subsequent getLaneBinder calls read the stale in-memory shadow (which is non-null, so the EventStore fallback is never reached). Evidence added by roles after terminal state will be in EventStore but invisible to the shadow.

smoking_guns.title: integrator.ts defines its own Binder type that diverges from binder.ts Binder
smoking_guns.detail: integrator.ts:36-46 defines a local Binder interface with fields like filesCreated, filesModified, laneId, campaignId, status, checkpointSha, executionEvents, diffs — this is a SUBSET of the canonical Binder type from binder.ts:84-123. It imports BinderService and type Binder (aliased as BinderBinder) from './binder' but uses its own Binder type for most internal logic. This creates a type divergence risk.

smoking_guns.title: No end-to-end test exercises the binder evidence chain
smoking_guns.detail: truth-closure.test.ts imports from secretary.ts but the pre-existing finding notes campaign layer has zero test coverage. The binder evidence chain (createBinder → addEvidence → updateStatus → finalizeBinder → reconstructBinder → getBinderDigest) has no integration test proving evidence integrity across the full lifecycle.

files_NOT_importing_binder_or_secretary: No files outside packages/opencode/src/campaign/ reference binder.ts or secretary.ts directly. The agent/machine/bootstrap.ts and index.ts reference './secretary' but those are the agent machine definition files (agent/machine/secretary.ts), NOT campaign/secretary.ts.
